description: Manual STRIDE analysis of the secure system (AWS Login Service with security controls applied)

threats.description: CORS is restricted to a specific allowlist of trusted origins. Residual risk remains if allowlist is misconfigured.
threats.mitigation: Review and audit the CORS allowlist regularly

threats.description: JWT tokens are signed with RS256 and validated on every request. Short expiry enforced.
threats.mitigation: Rotate signing keys periodically and monitor for stolen tokens

threats.description: Parameterized queries are used throughout. ORM prevents raw SQL construction.
threats.mitigation: Conduct regular security code reviews and use a WAF as defence-in-depth

threats.description: All authentication events are logged to a tamper-evident audit log with timestamps.
threats.mitigation: Periodically review audit log integrity and access controls

threats.description: Generic error messages returned. Enumeration risk significantly reduced.
threats.mitigation: Continue monitoring for differential response timing attacks

threats.description: Rate limiting and account lockout implemented. Risk reduced but not eliminated.
threats.mitigation: Add CAPTCHA for repeated failed attempts and monitor login anomalies

threats.description: Strong password policy enforced: minimum 12 characters, complexity requirements.
threats.mitigation: Add multi-factor authentication to further reduce privilege escalation risk
